items.subject: Pledge of Allegiance
items.publicContent: Board members, staff, and guests will recite the Pledge of Allegiance to open the meeting.

items.subject: Roll Call
items.publicContent: Board secretary will call the roll to establish a quorum.
items.staffContent: Quorum requires 4 of 7 members present. As of this notice, all 7 members have confirmed attendance.

items.subject: Approval of Agenda
items.publicContent: Motion to approve the agenda as presented, or with amendments.

items.subject: Approval of Minutes — March 18 Regular Board Meeting
items.publicContent: Approve the minutes from the March 18, 2026 Regular Board Meeting as presented.
items.staffContent: Minutes attached. No corrections noted by staff.

items.subject: Accounts Payable — March 2026
items.publicContent: Approve accounts payable for March 2026 totaling $1,247,832.
items.staffContent: AP register attached. Finance reviewed and approved all invoices. Three invoices over $50K flagged and reviewed separately.

items.subject: Personnel Report — March 2026
items.publicContent: Accept the personnel report for March 2026 as submitted by the Superintendent.
items.staffContent: Includes 3 new hires, 1 resignation, and 2 retirements. Full detail in attached report.

items.subject: Approval of Vendor Contracts under $25,000
items.publicContent: Approve routine vendor contracts under the $25,000 board approval threshold.
items.staffContent: List of 8 contracts attached. All reviewed by Purchasing and Legal.

items.subject: Acceptance of Donations
items.publicContent: Accept donations received in March 2026 totaling $14,500 from community partners.

items.subject: Approve 2026-27 Preliminary Budget
items.publicContent: Approve the 2026-27 Preliminary Budget as presented by the Superintendent and Director of Finance.
items.staffContent: Budget reflects a 3.2% increase in operational expenditures driven by salary step increases and rising utilities costs. Reserve fund remains above the 10% statutory minimum.
items.executiveContent: Confidential: projected budget shortfall of $420K in FY27 if enrollment decline continues. Contingency plan attached.

items.subject: First Reading — Policy 4.3 Travel and Expense Reimbursement
items.publicContent: First reading of proposed revisions to Policy 4.3 Travel and Expense Reimbursement. Second reading and action scheduled for May.
items.staffContent: Key changes: increased per diem rate to align with federal GSA rates, added requirement for receipts over $25, added language on ride-share services.

items.subject: Award Contract — HVAC Replacement, Building C
items.publicContent: Award contract for HVAC system replacement at Building C to the lowest responsive bidder.
items.staffContent: Bid opened April 8. Lowest responsive bid: Meridian Mechanical, $387,400. Second bid: Pacific Climate Systems, $412,200. Facilities reviewed both bids; recommends award to Meridian.

items.subject: Approve 2026-27 Substitute List
items.publicContent: Approve the 2026-27 classified and certificated substitute employee lists as presented by Human Resources.
items.staffContent: All candidates on the list have completed background checks, fingerprinting, and required onboarding paperwork.

items.subject: Roll Call
items.publicContent: Committee chair will call the roll.

items.subject: Review and Approval of Agenda
items.publicContent: Motion to approve the committee meeting agenda as presented.

items.subject: Q3 FY2026 Financial Report
items.publicContent: Review of third-quarter financial results compared to adopted budget. Year-to-date expenditures and revenues presented by the Director of Finance.
items.staffContent: YTD expenditures at 74.2% of budget — on track. Revenue at 71.8% — slightly behind due to delayed state apportionment. No major variances to flag.
items.executiveContent: Board-only note: enrollment tracking 1.8% below projection; if trend continues, we may need a mid-year budget revision in Q4.

items.subject: Capital Planning — 5-Year Infrastructure Outlook
items.publicContent: Discussion of the district's 5-year capital infrastructure plan, including deferred maintenance priorities, funding sources, and potential bond measures.
items.staffContent: Facilities assessment completed in February identified $8.2M in deferred maintenance across 6 sites. Presentation includes three funding scenarios.

items.subject: Revenue Projections — State Funding Update
items.publicContent: Update on state LCFF funding projections based on the Governor's revised budget proposal released April 10.
items.staffContent: Revised projection shows $340K less in LCFF than our adopted budget assumed. Finance recommends reducing discretionary reserves by $200K and identifying $140K in expenditure reductions.

items.subject: Expenditure Review — Major Object Codes YTD
items.publicContent: Review of year-to-date expenditures by major object code: salaries, benefits, books and supplies, services, capital outlay.
items.staffContent: Salaries tracking 1.4% over budget YTD due to more long-term substitute days than planned. Benefits on target. All other objects within budget.

items.subject: Proposed Budget Amendments — April 2026
items.publicContent: Recommend to the full board approval of April 2026 budget amendments as presented.
items.staffContent: Two amendments: (1) $78K transfer from contingency to Special Education support staff. (2) $24K additional from CTE grant for equipment purchase.

items.subject: Next Steps and Committee Recommendations
items.publicContent: Committee discussion of key takeaways and items to bring forward to the full board.

items.subject: Roll Call and Quorum
items.publicContent: Committee chair will call the roll and establish quorum.

items.subject: Approval of Previous Meeting Minutes
items.publicContent: Approve the minutes from the February 12, 2026 Audit & Risk Committee meeting.

items.subject: Internal Audit Charter Annual Review
items.publicContent: Annual review and reaffirmation of the Internal Audit Charter as required by professional standards.
items.staffContent: No substantive changes proposed. Minor wording update to Section 3 (scope) to clarify technology audit coverage.

items.subject: FY2026 Audit Plan Progress Report
items.publicContent: Status update on completion of the FY2026 internal audit plan.
items.staffContent: 4 of 7 planned audits complete. Payroll audit and IT General Controls audit in progress. Benefits audit deferred to FY27 due to resource constraints.

items.subject: Payroll Audit — Preliminary Findings
items.publicContent: Presentation of preliminary findings from the FY2026 payroll process audit.
items.staffContent: Three findings identified: (1) Time card approval delays in 12% of sampled records. (2) Two instances of pay rate changes without documented HR sign-off. (3) Overtime calculation error in legacy timesheet system — estimated $3,200 impact, now corrected.
items.executiveContent: Executive summary: no findings indicate systemic fraud risk. All findings have corrective actions assigned. Full report will be public at next meeting.

items.subject: Follow-up on Prior Audit Recommendations
items.publicContent: Status report on implementation of recommendations from prior internal audit reports.
items.staffContent: Of 18 open recommendations: 12 fully implemented, 4 in progress (on schedule), 2 overdue. Overdue items: Purchasing controls (assigned to Director of Finance, new target date June 30) and IT access review (assigned to IT Director, new target date May 15).

items.subject: Q1 FY2026 Risk Register Update
items.publicContent: Quarterly update to the district's enterprise risk register.
items.staffContent: Two new risks added: (1) Cybersecurity — phishing incident in March affected 3 staff accounts (contained, no data breach). (2) Enrollment decline — trending 2.1% below projection. Three risks downgraded following mitigation. Full register attached.

items.subject: Cybersecurity Incident Report — March 2026
items.publicContent: Briefing on the March 2026 phishing incident: scope, containment actions, and remediation steps.
items.staffContent: Phishing email targeted payroll staff. 3 accounts compromised; MFA prevented unauthorized transactions. IT reset credentials within 4 hours. No student or financial data accessed. Remediation: mandatory phishing training for all staff by May 1.
items.executiveContent: Full incident report and forensic summary provided to executive committee only. Insurance carrier notified per policy requirements.

items.subject: Insurance Coverage Review — Annual Update
items.publicContent: Annual review of district insurance coverage: property, liability, cyber, and workers' compensation.
items.staffContent: Cyber liability limit increased from $1M to $2M at renewal. All other lines renewed with no material changes. Total premium increase: 6.2%.

items.subject: Compliance Calendar — Upcoming Deadlines
items.publicContent: Review of upcoming statutory and regulatory compliance deadlines through Q2 FY2026.
items.staffContent: Key upcoming deadlines: LCAP annual update due June 30, external audit fieldwork scheduled July 14-25, annual Williams compliance report due September 1.

items.subject: External Audit Planning — Engagement Letter
items.publicContent: Authorize the Superintendent to execute the engagement letter with Eide Bailly LLP for the FY2026 external audit.
items.staffContent: Audit fee: $62,500, unchanged from FY2025. Fieldwork begins July 14. Preliminary results to committee in October.

items.subject: Committee Comments and Questions
items.publicContent: Open floor for committee member comments, questions, and items for future agendas.

items.subject: Next Meeting Date
items.publicContent: Confirm the date of the next Audit & Risk Committee meeting.
items.staffContent: Proposed: August 20, 2026, 3:00 PM.

items.subject: Adjournment
items.publicContent: Motion to adjourn the meeting.

items.subject: FY2027 Budget Development Timeline
items.publicContent: Overview of the FY2027 budget development schedule, key milestones, and board action dates.
items.staffContent: First interim due December 15. Budget adoption deadline June 15. Public hearing required by June 1.

items.subject: Enrollment Projections and Revenue Assumptions
items.publicContent: Workshop discussion on enrollment trends and their impact on LCFF revenue projections for FY2027.
items.staffContent: Demographer report attached. Three scenarios modeled: flat, -2%, -4% enrollment. Each has materially different revenue and staffing implications.

items.subject: Preliminary Budget Priorities
items.publicContent: Board and staff discussion of strategic budget priorities for FY2027 to inform preliminary budget development.
